name: Dept Of Labor
description_short: Federal credit union serving government employees and their families since 1935, offering checking, savings, loans, and ATM/debit card services.
description_long:
Dept of Labor Federal Credit Union is a federally chartered credit union headquartered in Washington, DC, established in 1935 with 91 years of operating history. The institution serves a membership base primarily composed of federal, state, and local government employees through its multiple common bond membership structure. With $146.56 million in total assets, 8,528 members, and 45 full and part-time employees, it operates two branch locations in Washington, DC, including its main office at 200 Constitution Ave NW.

The credit union offers comprehensive banking and financial services including checking and savings accounts, ATM/debit card programs, real estate loans, unsecured personal loans, and various other credit products. Their loan portfolio totals $103.4 million across multiple categories: $63.5 million in real estate loans, $12.2 million in unsecured loans, and $27.8 million in other loan types. They maintain FHLB membership status and provide services such as credit builder programs, first-time homebuyer assistance, low-cost wire transfers, and international remittances.

As a member-owned cooperative, Dept of Labor FCU distinguishes itself through its specialized focus on government sector employees and their families, maintaining membership requirements tied to federal, state, and local employment. The institution is insured by the National Credit Union Share Insurance Fund (NCUSIF) and operates under federal charter number 4963, with governance led by CEO Thomas Domingue and Chairperson Webster Coleman.

The credit union faces recent financial headwinds, reporting a net loss of $111,630 for the most recent reporting period despite generating $5.04 million in total income. This negative result reflects operational challenges with total expenses exceeding revenues, including $2.75 million in employee compensation and $2.26 million in other operating expenses, alongside an $804,302 provision for loan losses. Prospective members should consider their government employment eligibility and evaluate whether the institution's specialized focus and smaller asset base align with their banking needs. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Checking accounts (share drafts) with $33.2 million in deposits
Savings accounts (regular shares) with $34.3 million in member deposits
Real estate loans totaling $63.5 million in portfolio
Credit builder loans for credit development
ABA routing number 254074426 for direct deposit and electronic transactions
pros: 91-year operating history since 1935 demonstrates stability and institutional experience
FHLB membership provides access to liquidity and specialized credit products
NCUSIF deposit insurance protects member deposits under federal insurance framework
Offers first-time homebuyer programs and credit builder loans for membership development
Multiple branch locations in Washington, DC area with extended accessibility
Low-cost wire transfers and international remittance services reduce transaction fees
Member-owned cooperative structure aligns institution incentives with member interests
cons: Reported net loss of $111,630 in most recent period raises financial stability questions
Limited geographic footprint with only 2 branch locations restricted to Washington, DC
Membership restricted to government employees and common bond members, excluding general public
Relatively small asset base of $146.56 million limits product sophistication and service breadth
No investment products offered ($0 in total investments) restricts wealth management services
best_for: Federal, state, and local government employees seeking employer-aligned financial services
First-time homebuyers in the Washington, DC area with government employment
Credit union members seeking member-owned cooperative structure over commercial banking
Government sector employees with existing common bond eligibility for membership
diagnosis: Dept of Labor Federal Credit Union is best for government employees and their families in the Washington, DC area seeking member-owned banking with specialized government sector focus. Primary caveat: membership is restricted by employment requirements, recent financial losses warrant scrutiny of operational stability, and limited branch network restricts accessibility outside DC.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
